tools.name: admin.pubsubliteAdminProjectsLocationsOperationsCancel
tools.description: Starts asynchronous cancellation on a long-running operation. The server makes a best effort to cancel the operation, but success is not guaranteed. If the server doesn't support this method, it returns `google.rpc.Code.UNIMPLEMENTED`. Clients can use Operations.GetOperation or other methods to check whether the cancellation succeeded or whether the operation completed despite cancellation. On successful cancellation, the operation is not deleted; instead, it becomes an operation with an Operation.error value with a google.rpc.Status.code of 1, corresponding to `Code.CANCELLED`.

tools.name: admin.pubsubliteAdminProjectsLocationsOperationsList
tools.description: Lists operations that match the specified filter in the request. If the server doesn't support this method, it returns `UNIMPLEMENTED`.

tools.name: admin.pubsubliteAdminProjectsLocationsReservationsCreate
tools.description: Creates a new reservation.

tools.name: admin.pubsubliteAdminProjectsLocationsReservationsList
tools.description: Returns the list of reservations for the given project.

tools.name: admin.pubsubliteAdminProjectsLocationsReservationsTopicsList
tools.description: Lists the topics attached to the specified reservation.

tools.name: admin.pubsubliteAdminProjectsLocationsSubscriptionsCreate
tools.description: Creates a new subscription.

tools.name: admin.pubsubliteAdminProjectsLocationsSubscriptionsList
tools.description: Returns the list of subscriptions for the given project.

tools.name: admin.pubsubliteAdminProjectsLocationsSubscriptionsSeek
tools.description: Performs an out-of-band seek for a subscription to a specified target, which may be timestamps or named positions within the message backlog. Seek translates these targets to cursors for each partition and orchestrates subscribers to start consuming messages from these seek cursors. If an operation is returned, the seek has been registered and subscribers will eventually receive messages from the seek cursors (i.e. eventual consistency), as long as they are using a minimum supported client library version and not a system that tracks cursors independently of Pub/Sub Lite (e.g. Apache Beam, Dataflow, Spark). The seek operation will fail for unsupported clients. If clients would like to know when subscribers react to the seek (or not), they can poll the operation. The seek operation will succeed and complete once subscribers are ready to receive messages from the seek cursors for all partitions of the topic. This means that the seek operation will not complete until all subscribers come online. If the previous seek operation has not yet completed, it will be aborted and the new invocation of seek will supersede it.

tools.name: admin.pubsubliteAdminProjectsLocationsTopicsCreate
tools.description: Creates a new topic.

tools.name: admin.pubsubliteAdminProjectsLocationsTopicsDelete
tools.description: Deletes the specified topic.

tools.name: admin.pubsubliteAdminProjectsLocationsTopicsGet
tools.description: Returns the topic configuration.

tools.name: admin.pubsubliteAdminProjectsLocationsTopicsGetPartitions
tools.description: Returns the partition information for the requested topic.

tools.name: admin.pubsubliteAdminProjectsLocationsTopicsList
tools.description: Returns the list of topics for the given project.

tools.name: admin.pubsubliteAdminProjectsLocationsTopicsPatch
tools.description: Updates properties of the specified topic.

tools.name: admin.pubsubliteAdminProjectsLocationsTopicsSubscriptionsList
tools.description: Lists the subscriptions attached to the specified topic.

tools.name: cursor.pubsubliteCursorProjectsLocationsSubscriptionsCommitCursor
tools.description: Updates the committed cursor.

tools.name: cursor.pubsubliteCursorProjectsLocationsSubscriptionsCursorsList
tools.description: Returns all committed cursor information for a subscription.

tools.name: topicStats.pubsubliteTopicStatsProjectsLocationsTopicsComputeHeadCursor
tools.description: Compute the head cursor for the partition. The head cursor's offset is guaranteed to be less than or equal to all messages which have not yet been acknowledged as published, and greater than the offset of any message whose publish has already been acknowledged. It is zero if there have never been messages in the partition.

tools.name: topicStats.pubsubliteTopicStatsProjectsLocationsTopicsComputeMessageStats
tools.description: Compute statistics about a range of messages in a given topic and partition.

tools.name: topicStats.pubsubliteTopicStatsProjectsLocationsTopicsComputeTimeCursor
tools.description: Compute the corresponding cursor for a publish or event time in a topic partition.

tools.name: openapi.previewSpec
tools.description: Preview an OpenAPI document before adding it as a source

tools.name: openapi.addSource
tools.description: Add an OpenAPI source and register its operations as tools
